text.title: Trivy Pinned Supply-Chain Scan Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the Trivy CI gate, SBOM emission, and skip path.

rule.statement: CI MUST run BOTH `trivy fs` (over repo / IaC / lockfiles) AND `trivy image` (over built container images). Skipping either leaves a gap.
rule.rationale: `fs` catches deps + IaC misconfig; `image` catches base-image CVEs. Both are required.

rule.statement: Trivy job MUST fail on HIGH or CRITICAL CVE OR on any misconfig finding. PR merge MUST be blocked until resolved or explicitly waived.
rule.rationale: HIGH/CRITICAL are the minimum severity that warrants attention; MEDIUM/LOW can be advisory.

rule.statement: The Trivy GitHub Action MUST be referenced by full SHA (40-char), never by `@latest` or `@vX.Y`. The action SHA MUST be from a known-clean release.

rationale.source: GitHub Actions supply-chain audits + 2026 compromised-tag incidents

rule.rationale: Compromised tags ship malicious code under the same version; only SHA-pinning is safe.

rule.statement: The Trivy binary version in CI MUST be explicitly set to a known-clean release. `latest` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Same compromise vector as the action; pin the binary separately.

rule.statement: Every release tag MUST produce a CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM and attach it to the GitHub Release (via `gh release upload`). Compliance (EU CRA, US EO 14028) requires this.

rationale.source: EU CRA + US EO 14028 SBOM requirements

rule.rationale: SBOM is mandatory for distributable software in those jurisdictions.

rule.statement: The refs `aquasecurity/trivy@v0.69.4` and `aquasecurity/trivy-action@v0.30.0` MUST NEVER be used anywhere in any workflow or build step.
rule.rationale: These specific tags shipped malicious code; never use them.

rule.statement: If repo is docs-only OR is air-gapped with no offline-DB mirror OR is a notebook-only research repo, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when no supply-chain surface exists or scanner cannot run.
